w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Landing page / marketing website at fitosys.alchemetryx.com. Not the coach dashboard. Not the client intake form. This document governs only the public-facing marketing site.

Every section occupies exactly 100vh minimum height. No section breaks mid-viewport. On mobile, sections may extend beyond 100vh if content demands it — they must never compress below it. Use min-height: 100vh not height: 100vh to avoid overflow traps on mobile browsers with dynamic toolbars.
All sections live in one HTML document. Navigation anchors scroll to sections. No page reloads, no routing. This reduces bounce from slow connections — critical for Indian Tier 2 mobile users on 4G.

Nav stays fixed at top across all scroll positions. It becomes opaque with backdrop blur after 60px of scroll. Nav height is 64px on desktop, 56px on mobile. All section padding accounts for nav height so anchor links don't clip headings.

Defined stack from top to bottom: mobile menu overlay (999), nav bar (100), GSAP animated elements (10–50), section content (2), section backgrounds and decorative elements (0–1). No z-index values outside this system.

Each section contains one primary message, one supporting element, and one CTA. Nothing else. No secondary CTAs competing with the primary. No decorative elements that don't serve meaning. Every visual element must justify its presence.
80px, 10vw, 140px) top and bottom. Minimum horizontal gutter:
24px, 5vw, 80px). Content columns never exceed 1280px wide. Body copy columns never exceed 560px wide — long lines destroy readability on dark backgrounds.
Red (#F20000) appears on no more than 2–3 elements per section. It is reserved for CTAs, active states, and one key data point. It is never
used as a background fill for large areas except the CTA banner section. Every other surface is #0A0A0A, #111111, or #141414.
TR-08 — No images above the fold
hero section uses no raster images. Only CSS, SVG, and HTML-rendered UI
. This eliminates the largest LCP bottleneck. Images are only used in testimonial avatars (SVG initials acceptable as replacement) and optional photography sections below the fold.
TR-09 — One decorative element per section
One allowed per section: a grid background, a large ghost text watermark, a gradient glow, or a texture overlay. Never two in the same section. Never animated and static decorative elements together.
Two fonts only. Barlow Condensed (display) and Urbanist (body). No system fallback fonts rendered in production. Both loaded via Google Fonts with display=swap to prevent FOIT.

Maximum weight: 500. Minimum size: 12px. Always uppercase. Letter-spacing minimum +0.04em. Line-height minimum 1.05 for multi-line display text. Never used for body copy, captions, or anything below 12px.

All body copy, labels, buttons, nav links, form inputs, tags, and UI micro-text use Urbanist. Weights in use: 400 (body), 500 (labels), 600 (subheadings), 700 (CTAs and strong emphasis only). Line-height minimum 1.65 for paragraph text, 1.5 for short labels.
Defined scale only — no arbitrary font sizes. Scale: 11px (micro labels), 13px (small UI), 14px (captions), 16px (body), 18px (sub-body),
52px, 6vw, 96px) (hero title). Nothing outside this scale without a documented reason.
No body text below 14px rendered to users. No line-length above 70 characters for paragraph text. All paragraph text colour minimum #A0A0A0 on dark backgrounds — never below this contrast level.
TR-15 — GSAP as the sole animation library
All scroll-triggered animations, entrance effects, and micro-interactions use GSAP +
. No CSS @keyframes for scroll-triggered content (only for looping ambient animations like the ticker or bar pulse). No mixing of animation libraries.

Maximum 3 GSAP animations triggered per section on scroll entry. Stagger delays between sibling elements: 0.08s–0.15s. No animation duration above 1.2s. Ease functions: power3.out for entrances, power2.inOut for transitions,
All GSAP scroll animations must check prefers-reduced-motion. If set, skip animation and show elements at final state immediately. Implementation: wrap all
pain-points ticker uses pure CSS animation only (
keyframe). Never GSAP — it runs continuously and GSAP overhead on a looping element causes frame drops on low-end Android devices.
TR-19 — No animation on first paint
Hero text entrance begins at 200ms delay minimum after page load. No animation fires before fonts are confirmed loaded (
). This prevents FOUT-triggered layout shifts during animation.

transition (0.4s ease). GSAP is only used for the subtle pulse on float cards when tabs change. Auto-cycle interval is 5000ms. Interval is cleared immediately on any user tab interaction and never reinstated.
Paint): under 2.5s on a simulated 4G connection (25 Mbps). FID / INP: under 100ms. CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift): under 0.1. These are minimum pass thresholds — target green on
Insights for both mobile and desktop.
" and display=swap. GSAP loaded from CDN with defer. No synchronous scripts in <head>. CSS is inline in <style> block (single-file implementation) — no external stylesheet requests.
All photography below the fold uses loading="lazy". Maximum image width served: 1200px. Format:
with JPEG fallback. No image file above 200KB uncompressed. Testimonial avatars use CSS-rendered initials — zero image requests.
No analytics, chat widgets, or marketing pixels on the initial load. If analytics are added later (
or Plausible recommended — both privacy-first, lightweight), they load after
with a 3-second delay. No Google Tag Manager, no Meta Pixel, no Hotjar on this page.
TR-25 — Single HTTP request for CSS + JS
The marketing site is a single .html file. All CSS is inline. GSAP loads from a CDN. Target: total page weight under 150KB excluding fonts and GSAP library.

Three breakpoints only. Desktop: 1025px and above. Tablet: 769px–1024px. Mobile: 768px and below. No intermediate breakpoints. Grid layouts collapse from multi-column to single-column at the tablet breakpoint.
) for fluid scaling between mobile and desktop. No fixed pixel sizes for display text. Body text is fixed at 16px on mobile (minimum readable on small screens), 17px on desktop.

All interactive elements (buttons, nav links, tab switchers, form inputs) have a minimum touch target of 44×44px. This is Apple HIG standard and prevents mis-taps on mobile — relevant since Indian coaches are predominantly mobile users.

and float cards are hidden on screens below 1024px (display: none). The hero left column takes full width. This is intentional — a complex animated visual on a small screen creates cognitive overload and slows first paint.

overflow-x: hidden on both html and body. All absolutely positioned elements (float cards, decorative watermarks) are confirmed to not create horizontal overflow at any breakpoint. Test specifically on 375px (iPhone SE) and 390px (iPhone 14).

One <h1> per page (hero title). Section headings use <h2>. Supporting headings use <h3>. No heading hierarchy skipped. Navigation in <nav>. Footer in <footer>. Main content in <main>. This structure is required for crawler legibility and accessibility.
="https://fitosys.alchemetryx.com"> in <head>. Prevents duplicate content indexing if the site is accessible at multiple URLs (www vs non-www).

Title tag and meta description must include at least two of: "India", "Indian coaches", "WhatsApp", "coaching software India", "yoga coach software". These are the search terms your primary persona (Priya, Mumbai-based) actually types.
, offers (price in INR), description. This enables rich results in Google Search for software product pages.

All text on dark backgrounds meets WCAG AA minimum: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text (above 18px or 14px bold). Steel grey (#A0A0A0) on black (#0A0A0A) passes at 5.74:1. Red (#F20000) on black passes at 3.68:1 for display text only — never use red for small body copy.

All interactive elements are focusable via Tab key. Focus states are visible — styled with a 2px red outline offset by 3px (not the browser default). Mobile menu is operable without a mouse. Form fields have
> tag has a non-empty alt attribute. Decorative SVGs use aria-hidden="true". The phone
and dashboard UI are marked with role="
All form inputs are associated with <label> elements via for/id pairing. Required fields marked with aria-required="true". Error states use aria-
to link input to error message. The demo form is fully operable with screen readers.
. HTTP → HTTPS redirect enforced at the CDN level.
HTML: Cache-Control: no-cache (always fresh). CSS/JS assets: Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (content-hashed filenames). Fonts served by Google CDN — no control needed.
global CDN satisfies this by default. No additional uptime monitoring required for the marketing site at MVP stage.
Must render correctly on: Chrome 110+, Safari 16+, Firefox 115+, Samsung Internet 20+. Samsung Internet is the default browser on most Indian Android devices — it is not optional to test.

Physical device tests required before launch: iPhone 14 (390px), Samsung Galaxy A54 (412px — most common Indian mid-range), iPad Air (820px), MacBook 13". Simulator testing is not a substitute for physical device testing on font rendering and touch response.

before launch. Pass criteria: Performance 85+, Accessibility 90+, Best Practices 90+, SEO 95+. Run on both mobile and desktop simulation. Document the score and any items below threshold with a resolution plan.
All GSAP animations must maintain 60fps on a mid-range Android device (Snapdragon 680 class). Test using Chrome
Performance tab. Frame drops below 50fps during scroll animations are a blocking issue.
WCAG AA contrast on all text
is specific, testable, and tied to either the brand, the audience, or the stack. Nothing is vague.
Two I'd flag as the most commonly missed in practice:
(reduced motion) gets skipped on almost every GSAP-heavy site and becomes an accessibility liability, and
test on Chrome and miss Samsung-specific rendering differences entirely.
